title: draba glacialis var. incompta
intro: Draba glacialis var. incompta, also known as Alpine Whitlow Grass, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antioxidant. Alpine Whitlow Grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
